title: A7. Dockerfile + docker compose for local/scheduled runs
description:
Optional container path for running FlatPilot without installing Python 3.11+ and Playwright browsers on the host. Phase 5 will add more services (web UI, worker, DB), but for Phase 1 this is a single-service compose.yml that makes the CLI reproducible and schedulable.

Scope:
- Dockerfile based on the official Playwright Python image (Chromium pre-installed with system deps).
- Installs the flatpilot package via pip install -e '.[dev]' so code changes are picked up.
- docker-compose.yml with one service 'flatpilot'.
- Host volume mount for ~/.flatpilot so SQLite DB, sessions, and geocode cache persist across container restarts.
- .env support for TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, SMTP creds, etc.
- .dockerignore to keep the build context small.
- README section documenting: docker compose run --rm flatpilot init, docker compose run --rm flatpilot run, etc.
- Local pip-install path stays working; Docker is an alternative, not a replacement.

title: E3. Radius filter wired into E1
description: Add radius filter to E1 using E2: flat distance <= profile.radius_km, with district allowlist as an optional additional constraint layered on top.

title: C3. stats helpers
description: get_stats() returns: total flats, new-in-last-24h, matched, notified, rejected-by-reason histogram. Used by `flatpilot status` and the dashboard.

title: F. Notifications (Phase 1)
description: Telegram + email; dedup per flat/channel.

title: E1. Deterministic filter module
description: matcher/filters.py: rent band (warm only), rooms band, WBS compatibility (flat requires_wbs -> profile must have wbs=yes with compatible size_category and income_category), district allowlist (if set), household size (flat hints / description), pets (flat pets_allowed vs profile pets), move-in date (flat available_from <= profile.move_in_date + tolerance), furnished pref, min-contract-months. Pure functions, each returns (passes: bool, reason: str|None).

title: D2. WG-Gesucht Wohnungen scraper
description: Parameterized by profile.city + profile.radius_km. Parses search results list + detail page. Extracts all C1 fields. Dedup by WG-Gesucht listing id. Respects the D0 decision on cadence and cookie strategy.

title: F1. Telegram adapter
description: HTTPS sendMessage against the Telegram Bot API. Bot token from env (TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN), chat_id from profile. No polling, no hosting.

title: D4. `flatpilot scrape` CLI command
description: `flatpilot scrape [--platform wg-gesucht] [--watch --interval SECS]`. Single pass by default; --watch runs a loop with graceful Ctrl-C.

title: E4. `flatpilot match` command + matches writes
description: `flatpilot match` reads the flats table, runs all filters, writes matches rows (one per (flat, profile_hash, decision)) with full decision_reasons_json. Idempotent: rerunning against the same flats does not duplicate rows.

title: D. Scraper Framework + WG-Gesucht (Phase 1 MVP)
description: Scraper protocol + first scraper. WG-Gesucht Wohnungen (full flats, not WGs), any German city + radius.

title: H1. view.py dashboard generator
description: Generates a single HTML file at ~/.flatpilot/dashboard.html. Groups matched flats by decision (Matched / Rejected-this-session / Rejected-historical). Filters: district, rent range (slider), rooms, WBS required y/n. No LLM scores.

title: C2. matches table schema
description: Columns: flat_id (fk), profile_version_hash, decision (match|reject|skipped), decision_reasons_json, decided_at, notified_at (nullable), notified_channels_json. One row per (flat_id, profile_version_hash, decision).

title: G4. Rotating file logging
description: Logs to ~/.flatpilot/logs/flatpilot.log with daily rotation (stdlib RotatingFileHandler or TimedRotatingFileHandler). Console mirror at INFO.

title: D0. Anti-bot survival spike (1 day, blocks D2)
description: Time-boxed investigation: can we poll WG-Gesucht Wohnungen search every 60-120 s for 4 hours without tripping hCaptcha, using Playwright + realistic UA + cookie warm-up? If blocked, enumerate mitigations (lower cadence, longer cookie warm-up, residential IP, RSS-style email fallback) before committing to the scraper design.

title: G3. `flatpilot status` implementation
description: Prints DB stats (from C3) plus last-run timestamp and total notifications sent per channel.

title: E. Matcher (Phase 1)
description: Pure-Python deterministic hard filters. No LLM. <=150 LOC of filter logic.

title: F2. Email adapter (SMTP)
description: SMTP via stdlib smtplib, creds from env (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_USER, SMTP_PASS, SMTP_FROM). Plain+HTML multipart.

title: G. Orchestrator & run loop (Phase 1)
description: `flatpilot run` stitches scrape -> match -> notify together.

title: E2. Distance (Haversine + Nominatim geocoding cache)
description: matcher/distance.py: Haversine from profile.home_{lat,lng} to flat.{lat,lng}. Flat coordinates come from (a) scraper if the platform provides them, (b) Nominatim geocode of the address with on-disk cache at ~/.flatpilot/geocode_cache.json (180-day TTL), (c) city/district centroid fallback. Politeness: 1 req/s to Nominatim, custom User-Agent.

title: D1. Scraper protocol + registry
description: `flatpilot.scrapers.base.Scraper` protocol: fetch_new(profile) -> Iterable[Flat], platform: str, user-agent, cookie jar path. Registry pattern so future scrapers register themselves and the CLI can list/target them.

title: H3. `flatpilot dashboard` command
description: Generates dashboard and opens it in the default browser (webbrowser.open).

title: D3. Rate-limit + cookie persistence
description: Configurable interval (default 120 s), 429 backoff, polite UA, cookie jar at ~/.flatpilot/sessions/wg-gesucht/. One cookie warm-up at startup.

title: H. HTML Dashboard (Phase 1)
description: Self-contained HTML report of matches. No backend server.

title: H2. Per-card Open / Copy Link
description: Each card has an 'Open listing' link to the external URL and a 'Copy link' JS button. No Apply button in MVP (Phase 3 adds it).

title: F3. Channel dispatcher with per-flat dedup
description: notifications/dispatcher.py: for each matched flat, iterate enabled channels, skip if matches.notified_channels already includes that channel. Update notified_at + notified_channels on success. Failures: log + retry on next run.

title: F5. `flatpilot notify --test` command
description: Sends one synthetic flat to both configured channels for sanity-checking credentials.

title: G2. `flatpilot run --watch --interval 120`
description: Poll loop with graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM. Logs each pass summary.

title: G1. `flatpilot run` single-pass
description: Orchestrates all three stages sequentially: scrape (all enabled platforms), match, notify. Exit code reflects errors.

title: F4. Message template
description: Plain + HTML templates including title, Warmmiete, rooms, district, online_since, listing URL. Single template module so both channels share formatting.

title: C. Data Model (Phase 1)
description: SQLite schema for flats and matches; forward-migration support.

title: A3. config.py with ~/.flatpilot paths
description: Module defining APP_DIR (~/.flatpilot, overridable via FLATPILOT_DIR env), DB_PATH, PROFILE_PATH, SESSIONS_DIR, GEOCODE_CACHE_PATH, LOG_DIR, ENV_PATH. ensure_dirs() creates them. load_env() loads .env from APP_DIR then cwd as fallback. Written from scratch, no copy-paste from any other project.

title: FlatPilot MVP — German flat-hunting agent
description: Clean-room, MIT-licensed agent that scrapes German flat listings, deterministically matches them against a user profile, and notifies the user via Telegram / email. Germany-only (MVP), any German city the user configures plus a radius in km. No LLM in Phase 1. Phasing: 0 foundation, 1 MVP (WG-Gesucht + match + notify + HTML dashboard), 2 Kleinanzeigen + cross-platform dedup, 3 inberlinwohnen + apply button + tracking UI, 4 ImmoScout RSS + opt-in auto-apply, 5 multi-user web UI.

title: A4. database.py with thread-local SQLite + forward-migration helper
description: SQLite connection helper using WAL mode, per-thread cached connections, Row factory. init_db() creates schema via CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS. ensure_columns() compares PRAGMA table_info vs a column registry and ALTER TABLEs any missing. Written fresh, no lines copied from any other project.

title: C1. flats table schema
description: Columns: external_id, platform, listing_url, title, rent_warm_eur, rent_cold_eur, extra_costs_eur, rooms (float), size_sqm, address, district, lat, lng, online_since (ts), available_from (date|null), requires_wbs (bool), wbs_size_category (int|null), wbs_income_category (int|null), furnished (bool|null), deposit_eur (int|null), min_contract_months (int|null), pets_allowed (bool|null), description (text), scraped_at (ts), first_seen_at (ts), canonical_flat_id (int|null, for cross-platform dedup in Phase 2). UNIQUE (platform, external_id).

title: A2. pyproject.toml + dependencies
description: pyproject.toml declaring package `flatpilot`, Python 3.11+, runtime deps (typer, rich, httpx, beautifulsoup4, playwright, python-dotenv, pyyaml, pydantic, geopy), dev deps (pytest, ruff). Entry point `flatpilot = flatpilot.cli:app`.

title: A1. Create package skeleton (flatpilot/)
description: Create the Python package layout: src/flatpilot/ with __init__.py, __main__.py, empty submodule dirs (wizard/, scrapers/, matcher/, notifications/) each with __init__.py. No logic, just the shape.

title: B3. profile.example.json shipped with package
description: Example profile: Frankfurt am Main, 25 km, 1000-1500 EUR warm, no WBS, 2-3 rooms, employed, 3000 EUR net, 2026-06-01 move-in. Referenced by tests and docs.

title: B1. Profile schema (pydantic)
description: pydantic model for profile.json: rent_min_warm / rent_max_warm (EUR int), wbs: {status: none|yes, size_category: 1-5+, income_category: 100|140|160|180}, city (str), radius_km (int), district_allowlist (list[str], optional), home_lat / home_lng (float, filled after geocode), rooms_min / rooms_max (int), household_size (int), kids (int), pets (list[str]), status (student|employed|self_employed|other), net_income_eur (int), move_in_date (date), smoker (bool), furnished_pref (any|furnished|unfurnished), min_contract_months (int|null), notifications: {telegram: {enabled, bot_token_env, chat_id}, email: {enabled, smtp_env}}. country pinned to 'DE'.

title: A5. cli.py skeleton with typer commands
description: typer app with placeholder commands: init, doctor, run, scrape, match, notify, status, dashboard. Each just prints 'not implemented' for now; real implementations land in later tasks.

title: B2. init wizard with conditional WBS branch
description: `flatpilot init` drives a rich.Prompt flow: personal+housing fields, conditional WBS branch only if user says 'yes' to WBS, geocode city via Nominatim (with explicit confirm step showing resolved coordinates), re-run preserves existing profile fields. Writes ~/.flatpilot/profile.json.

title: B. Setup Wizard & Profile (Phase 1)
description: First-time setup wizard that produces ~/.flatpilot/profile.json. Germany-wide city + radius, WBS-aware.

title: A6. doctor command
description: `flatpilot doctor` checks: Python >= 3.11, ~/.flatpilot writable, Playwright browsers present (`playwright install chromium` prompt if not), optional Telegram creds (BOT_TOKEN + CHAT_ID) and SMTP creds in .env. Shows OK / MISSING / optional per check.

title: A. Project Foundation (Phase 0)
description: New clean-room repo scaffolding for FlatPilot. Prereq for every other sub-epic.

title: I-bis-1. Address normalization + fuzzy match
description: Normalize address (lowercase, strip punctuation, 'Straße' <-> 'Str.'). Fuzzy match key = (normalized_address, rent_warm +- 50 EUR, size +- 3 qm). Store canonical_flat_id on flats; keep all source listing URLs.

title: I-bis. Cross-platform dedup (Phase 2)
description: Same flat posted on WG-Gesucht AND Kleinanzeigen should produce ONE notification. Triggered when a second scraper lands.

title: I1. Kleinanzeigen Wohnung-miete scraper
description: Search Wohnung category with city + radius, parse price / Warm-vs-Kaltmiete disambiguation, Zimmer, qm, Stadtteil. Same output shape as D2.

title: I2. Anti-bot handling + rate limit
description: Stealth Playwright, residential-UA pool, backoff on captcha indicators, cookie jar under ~/.flatpilot/sessions/kleinanzeigen/.

title: I. Kleinanzeigen scraper (Phase 2)
description: Second scraper. Playwright-stealth mode; aggressive anti-bot. Germany-wide.

title: I-bis-2. Matcher + notifier keyed on canonical
description: Matcher writes one match per canonical flat. Notifier keyed on canonical flat_id so cross-platform duplicates trigger only one ping.

title: Test Task 1
description: child 1

title: Test Task 2
description: child 2

title: Test Epic
description: parent

title: Test
description: test

title: Test
description: test

title: K. ImmoScout24 RSS (Phase 4)
description: NO scraping. User supplies saved-search RSS feed URLs in their profile.

title: J2. City gating in registry
description: Scraper registry: each scraper declares supported cities (or 'all'). Matcher/orchestrator skip scrapers whose supported cities don't include profile.city.

title: L3. WG-Gesucht Playwright form filler
description: Navigate to the flat's contact form, fill fields, attach files, dry-run mode to preview without submitting.

title: N3. Cooldown between submits per platform
description: Default 120 s; enforced at dispatch time.

title: M2. Matches tab: Apply / Skip / View buttons
description: Apply triggers L4 via a local helper (CLI spawn). Skip writes matches.decision=skipped. View opens the listing.

title: M1. Tabs UI
description: Extend H1 dashboard with tabbed view.

title: M. Applications Tracking UI (Phase 3)
description: Three tabs in the HTML dashboard: Matches / Applied / Responses.

title: L4. `flatpilot apply <flat_id>` command
description: CLI entry that runs L3 for the given flat. --dry-run preview. Writes an applications row with status submitted/failed/reason.

title: L5. Attachment store
description: ~/.flatpilot/attachments/ holds SCHUFA, Gehaltsnachweise, ID scans etc. Profile names which file to attach for each platform.

title: N6. Integration tests for caps/cooldowns/pause
description: Verify each safety rail with seeded DB and time travel.

title: M4. Responses tab with paste-reply form
description: Textarea to paste landlord reply -> updates status + response_text. Client-side only; writes to DB via a small local helper.

title: N. Opt-in Auto-Apply with safety rails (Phase 4)
description: Per-saved-search auto-apply; daily caps, cooldowns, pause switch.

title: K1. RSS parser adapter
description: Consume feedparser output. Map RSS items into Flat records. Limited fields available from RSS -> optional detail-page enrichment pass behind a flag.

title: N2. Daily cap per platform
description: Default 20/day; enforced by querying applications.

title: L. Per-flat Apply Button (Phase 3)
description: CLI + UI button to contact the landlord for a specific flat. Starts with WG-Gesucht contact form. Per-site Playwright fillers.

title: J. inberlinwohnen.de scraper (Phase 3)
description: Berlin municipal housing scraper. Opt-in: only runs if profile.city == 'Berlin'. WBS-heavy.

title: N1. Saved-search schema with auto_apply toggle
description: Profile gains a saved_searches list, each with its own filters overlay and an auto_apply: bool.

title: N5. Global PAUSE switch
description: Presence of ~/.flatpilot/PAUSE halts all auto-apply immediately. Mention in doctor.

title: M3. Applied tab with status badges
description: Shows applications rows sorted by applied_at; status badges; filter by status.

title: L2. Anschreiben template composer
description: Template-based Anschreiben per platform. User maintains templates at ~/.flatpilot/templates/<platform>.md. Fills profile placeholders. AI-generated variants are explicitly out of scope for Phase 3.

title: J1. inberlinwohnen Wohnungen scraper
description: Parse listings. Flag requires_wbs=True by default (most are). Extract WBS size + income constraints when stated.

title: N4. Skip-and-log on missing field/attachment
description: Auto-apply never submits incomplete applications. Failed attempts log with reason and are visible in the Applied tab.

title: L1. applications table schema
description: Columns: flat_id, platform, listing_url, title, rent_warm, rooms, size_sqm, district, applied_at, method (manual|auto), message_sent (text), attachments_sent_json, status (submitted|failed|viewing_invited|rejected|no_response), response_received_at, response_text, notes.

title: O. Web UI (multi-user, Phase 5)
description: FastAPI backend + React/Next.js frontend. Per-user profiles, magic-link auth, SQLite -> Postgres migration path.

title: O3. React/Next.js tabs
description: Port Matches / Applied / Responses tabs from the HTML dashboard.

title: O4. Docker-compose + deployment docs
description: One-command local stack; deployment instructions for a VPS.

title: O1. Architecture decision record
description: Decide: FastAPI + Next.js; magic-link auth; SQLite -> Postgres migration path; deployment story (docker-compose starter).

title: O2. Per-user isolation in data model
description: All tables gain user_id; row-level scoping; migration from single-user state.

value: Never push directly to main. Every change goes through a PR: git fetch origin; git checkout -b <type>/<slug> origin/main; commit; git push -u origin <branch>; gh pr create --base main --head <branch> --fill. Return the PR URL and stop. Force-push allowed only on personal feature branches that nobody else has pulled (--force-with-lease).
No AI co-author trailers in commits. No 'Co-Authored-By: Claude ...', no '🤖 Generated with Claude Code'. Commits are authored by the human only.

Clean-room policy: FlatPilot is independent MIT-licensed. Never copy code from any other project (including any job-hunting agent that may have inspired the high-level architecture). Read outside code only as a design reference; always rewrite from scratch.

Commit author for this repo: Mukhammad Ibrokhimov <ibrohimovmuhammad2020@gmail.com>. In a fresh clone: git config user.email ibrohimovmuhammad2020@gmail.com && git config user.name 'Mukhammad Ibrokhimov'. Any other email on the machine is NOT for commits here.

For FlatPilot, batch several small beads tasks into one PR instead of opening a separate PR per task. One commit per task on the same branch (each commit message references its beads ID), one PR that closes multiple beads issues. Large or risky tasks still get their own PR. Why: the user said so after A7 merged — opening a PR for every small scaffold task is unnecessary churn. How to apply: if a task is small (single file, no architectural decision, < ~100 LOC), put it on the same feature branch as the previous/next small task; rename the branch to reflect the bundle (e.g. chore/a3-a5-foundation). Only open the PR after the batch is done.
